description: When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE Applies only to the Aruba 6300 Switch Series. In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped. Scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet. Workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.
symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.
scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet.
workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

description: Symptom: The uplink port gets blocked. Scenario: An STP-aware switch is connected to another STP-aware switch with an intermediate STP-unaware switch that has VLAN mismatches. Workaround: Use the following steps to resolve: 1. Configure bpdu-filter on the blocked port (or) 2. Correct the mismatch VLANs (or) 3. Enable STP in the intermediate STP-unaware switch

description: Symptom: The entire stack reboots and zeroizes when only a single member should do so. Scenario: This issue occurs when adding a new member in standard secure mode to a stack in enhanced secure mode. Workaround: Configure the new member to enhanced secure mode before joining the stack.

workaround: Configure the new member to enhanced secure mode before joining the stack.

description: Symptom: The switch continues to query the external DNS server (1.1.1.1) even though internal DNS servers are already configured. Scenario: This issue occurs when the TACACS server is specified as an FQDN within the management VRF, the DNS server is also configured in the same VRF, and TACACS authorization is enabled. Workaround: Log out and back in to the switch console.

description: Symptom: The DHCP Client is not getting an IP address. Scenario: This issue occurs when the clients hardware address and the Ethernet packet source MAC address are different. Workaround: Use dhcpv4-snooping option 82 untrusted-policy replace or configure a static MAC entry for the client hardware address.
symptom: The DHCP Client is not getting an IP address.
scenario: This issue occurs when the clients hardware address and the Ethernet packet source MAC address are different.
workaround: Use dhcpv4-snooping option 82 untrusted-policy replace or configure a static MAC entry for the client hardware address.

workaround: Use dhcpv4-snooping option 82 untrusted-policy replace or configure a static MAC entry for the client hardware address.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what symptom occurs in DHCP Snooping Bug 400570?
DHCP Snooping (Bug ID 400570): The symptom is: The DHCP Client is not getting an IP address.

description: Symptom: Attempting to configure a class with a long name can result in errors such as "% Command failed," despite the same name working on a previous version. Scenario: This issue occurs when attempting to create a class with a name longer than 64 characters or have a class and DUR name with a combined length greater than 64 characters. Workaround: Use shorter class and/or DUR names.

description: Symptom: Devices with 80k static neighbor entries fail to boot after 30 minutes. Scenario: This issue occurs when attempting to boot a device with 80k static neighbors or attempting to copy a checkpoint with those characteristics to running configuration. Workaround: Reduce the scale or use the fixed firmware version.

description: Symptom: Traffic received on a UBT Wake-on-LAN tunnel may not reach the intended client, resulting in silent clients failing to respond to network requests. Scenario: This issue may occur when multicast or broadcast packets are not forwarded to the client due to internal port handling logic.
symptom: Traffic received on a UBT Wake-on-LAN tunnel may not reach the intended client, resulting in silent clients failing to respond to network requests.
scenario: This issue may occur when multicast or broadcast packets are not forwarded to the client due to internal port handling logic.

description: Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting
symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems.
scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools.
workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting

description: Symptom: In a VXLAN environment, AOS-CXswitches may fails to install routes in the routing table, causing traffic loss for certain destinations. Scenario: This issue may occur during migration or configuration changes involving SVI and Routing enablement on VSX pairs. Workaround: Restart the hpe-routing process using the following: Copy switch# start-shell

description: Symptom: For an existing 6in4 tunnel that was up and running, if the source IP is deleted and then re-added, the 6in4 tunnel does not come up, even though the destination IP address is on the IP routing table. The same behavior happens for GRE tunnels, but does not happen for 6in6 tunnels. Scenario: This issue occurs when a source IP address is deleted and re-added for a GRE or 6in4 tunnel. It reamins down with permanent packet loss for packets that use that tunnel.
